lines.target: Lets meet our friends the Wilsons again.
lines.base: De nuevo nos encontramos con nuestros amigos los Wilson.
lines.pronunciation: lets mit... frends... éguein. — 3 ... risivs kools... — 5 ... bisii... kélig. — 6 ... deili... hiudeh.

lines.target: At the office, David has a lot of work.
lines.base: En la oficina, David tiene mucho trabajo.

lines.target: He receives calls from people who phone to offer him information.
lines.base: Recibe llamadas de gente que telefonea para ofrecerle información.

lines.target: If he can, he goes out to see them,
but if he is too busy, he sends a colleague.
lines.base: Pero si está demasiado ocupado, envía a un compañero.

lines.target: Because his is a daily paper, the amount of work is huge.
lines.base: Porque su periódico es un periódico diario; la cantidad de trabajo es enorme.

lines.target: At her office, Joan types her boss's letters and answers the phone.
lines.base: En la oficina, Joan escribe a máquina las cartas de su jefe y contesta al teléfono.

lines.target: She only works part-time, so she goes home at half past twelve.
lines.base: Trabaja sólo media jornada, así que vuelve a casa a las doce y media.

lines.target: Then she does the housework, because they cannot afford a “ help ”.
lines.base: Entonces hace las faenas de la casa, porque no pueden permitirse una asistenta.

lines.target: When she finishes, she makes a cup of tea and reads a magazine.
lines.base: Cuando termina, se prepara una taza de té y lee una revista.

lines.target: Then she starts preparing David's dinner.
lines.base: Luego empieza a preparar la cena de David.

lines.target: David usually arrives home about half-past Six,
lines.base: David, normalmente, ¡lega a casa hacia las seis y media,

lines.target: but sometimes he works late and does not arrive home until nine o'clock.
lines.base: pero a veces trabaja tarde y no llega a casa hasta las nueve.

gap_candidates.target: but if he is too busy, he sends a colleague. (4)
Because his is a daily paper, the amount of work is huge. (5)
At her office, Joan types her boss's letters and answers the phone. (6)
Then she does the housework, because they cannot afford a “ help ”. (8)
When she finishes, she makes a cup of tea and reads a magazine.
Then she starts preparing David's dinner.
David usually arrives home about half-past Six,
